response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which made his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. Their total collection was 76 blueberries.

***

They used all their blueberries to make muffins. One batch required 9 blueberries. They made 8 batches of muffins using all 72 blueberries. They had 4 blueberries left over. Each batch sold for $5. They sold 8 batches. Their total earnings from selling the muffins were $40.

***

They had some blueberries left after making the muffins. They did not use those for more batches. The leftover blueberries were not sold. The money they made came only from the muffins they sold. Their total money was from the 8 batches they made.

***

They worked together to collect and use the blueberries. Their effort led to the creation of muffins and the sale of those muffins. The money they earned was a result of their combined work. Their youth showed through their teamwork and dedication.
